content: Confirm task is Level 3 by checking `memory-bank/tasks.md`.
Ensure core Memory Bank structure and paths are known (AI should have internalized from `main` rule).
Load Level 3 specific planning (`Level3/planning-comprehensive.mdc`) and task tracking (`Level3/task-tracking-intermediate.mdc`) rules.
Review `memory-bank/projectbrief.md`: Briefly note the new feature if it impacts the overall brief.
Ensure `memory-bank/tasks.md` is ready for the detailed planning sections outlined in `Level3/planning-comprehensive.mdc`.
Define detailed functional and non-functional requirements for the feature.
Perform component analysis: identify new components to build and existing ones that will be modified. Map their interactions.
Develop an implementation strategy: outline the main steps or stages for building the feature.
Identify dependencies (technical, data, other features) and potential risks, along with mitigation ideas.
Explicitly identify and flag parts of the feature that require CREATIVE mode (e.g., specific UI/UX challenges, new architectural patterns for the feature, complex algorithms).
Document the complete plan (requirements, components, strategy, dependencies, risks, creative flags) in `memory-bank/tasks.md` under the Level 3 feature task entry.

For each aspect flagged in `tasks.md` for creative exploration: a. Load relevant `creative-phase-*.mdc` rule (e.g., UI/UX, architecture). b. Define the problem, explore options, analyze trade-offs. c. Make a design decision and document it with rationale in a new `memory-bank/creative-[aspect_name].md` file. d. Update `tasks.md`: mark the creative sub-task as complete and link to the decision document.
Thoroughly review the feature plan in `memory-bank/tasks.md` and all relevant `memory-bank/creative-*.md` decision documents.
Set up the development environment (new branch, install any new dependencies, configure tools).
Implement the feature, building out modules/components as planned. Prioritize clean code and adherence to design specifications.
Perform unit tests for new logic and integration tests as components are assembled.
Ensure all UI elements strictly follow `memory-bank/style-guide.md`.
Update `memory-bank/tasks.md` with progress on sub-tasks, and `memory-bank/progress.md` with details of implemented parts, commands used, and any significant findings.
Conduct end-to-end testing of the completed feature against its requirements.
Review the entire feature development lifecycle: initial requirements, plan, creative designs, implementation, and testing outcomes.
Analyze what went well, what was challenging, and any deviations from the original plan or design.
Document key lessons learned regarding technology, architecture, process, or team collaboration relevant to this feature.
Specifically assess how effective the creative phase decisions were during actual implementation.
Create the `memory-bank/reflection-[feature_id].md` document.
Update `memory-bank/tasks.md` to mark the reflection stage for the feature as complete.
Consolidate all documentation related to the feature: the plan section from `tasks.md`, all `creative-*.md` files, the `reflection-*.md` file, and relevant summaries from `progress.md`.

Create a dedicated feature archive summary document in `memory-bank/archive/feature-[feature_id]_YYYYMMDD.md`. This summary should briefly describe the feature, its purpose, key decisions, and link to the more detailed documents.
Update `memory-bank/tasks.md` to mark the entire Level 3 feature task as "COMPLETED" and "ARCHIVED," providing a link to the new archive summary.
Update `memory-bank/activeContext.md` to clear information related to the completed feature, preparing for the next task.

Level 3 tasks build significant features. Balance detailed planning and targeted design with efficient execution. Document key decisions and outcomes to ensure the feature is understandable and maintainable.
